tensile_astm.name: Tensile Test (ASTM)
tensile_astm.purpose: Determine yield strength, tensile strength, and elongation of structural steels per ASTM.

prep: Machine specimen to ASTM E8 dimensions appropriate for product form and thickness.
Remove surface defects and burrs without altering cross-section.
Measure and record original gauge length and cross-sectional area.

procedure_steps: Mount the specimen in the tensile testing machine grips with proper alignment to avoid bending.
Attach extensometer if required for yield and elongation measurement.
Set crosshead speed or strain rate according to ASTM E8 for the chosen method.
Zero the load and extensometer before loading.
Apply tensile load continuously at the specified rate until fracture.
Determine yield strength (0.2% offset or as specified), ultimate tensile strength, and elongation.
Fit the broken ends together and measure final gauge length to calculate percent elongation.
Visually inspect fracture surface and note ductile or brittle appearance.

impact_astm.name: Charpy V-Notch Impact Test (ASTM)
impact_astm.purpose: Measure impact toughness and resistance to brittle fracture.

prep: Machine specimens to ASTM E23 dimensions with specified notch geometry.
Ensure notch is properly machined and free from burrs.
Condition specimens at the specified test temperature for sufficient time to stabilize.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and energy range of the impact testing machine.
Place the specimen in the anvil with the notch facing away from the striker.
Release the pendulum to fracture the specimen in a single blow.
Record absorbed energy from the machine readout.
Repeat for the required number of specimens and calculate average absorbed energy.
Report test temperature and average impact energy.

bend_astm.name: Bend Test (ASTM)
bend_astm.purpose: Evaluate ductility and ability to withstand bending without cracking.

prep: Cut specimens to required width and thickness according to product or project specification.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches or sharp defects.
Record thickness and orientation (longitudinal or transverse).

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate bend fixture and mandrel diameter based on specimen thickness and standard requirements.
Place the specimen on the supports with the surface to be evaluated in tension.
Bend the specimen to the specified angle (often 180 degrees) around the mandrel.
Remove the specimen and visually inspect the tension surface under good lighting.
Record any cracks, tears, or surface discontinuities and their lengths.
Evaluate results against acceptance criteria in the relevant product or welding standard.

hardness_astm.name: Hardness Test (ASTM)
hardness_astm.purpose: Verify material consistency and support strength correlation.
procedure_steps: Prepare a clean, flat, and smooth surface on the specimen by grinding or machining.
For Brinell (ASTM E10): select appropriate ball diameter and test force based on material and thickness.
Apply the test force for the specified dwell time, then remove the force.
Measure indentation diameter in at least two perpendicular directions and calculate Brinell hardness.
For Rockwell (ASTM E18): select appropriate scale, apply minor load, then major load, and read hardness directly.
Record hardness values and compare with typical or specified ranges for the grade.

chemical_astm.name: Chemical Composition (ASTM)
chemical_astm.purpose: Determine chemical composition and verify compliance with grade limits.
procedure_steps: Select a representative sample from the heat, plate, or shape.
Grind or mill the surface to remove scale, rust, and contamination.
Place the sample on the spectrometer stand ensuring good electrical contact.
Calibrate the instrument using certified reference materials.
Perform multiple sparks according to instrument procedure.
Record chemical composition for required elements and compare with the relevant ASTM product standard.

weld_qualification_astm.name: Weld Procedure / Welder Qualification
weld_qualification_astm.purpose: Qualify welding procedures and welders for structural steel.
procedure_steps: Prepare weld test coupons according to the WPS and AWS D1.1 requirements.
Perform welding using specified process, consumables, preheat, and interpass temperatures.
Visually inspect welds for surface defects.
Section coupons for macroetch examination; etch and examine fusion, penetration, and weld profile.
Prepare bend specimens (face, root, side) from the weld and perform bend tests per AWS D1.1 and ASTM A370.
Prepare tensile specimens from the weld or HAZ if required and test per ASTM A370.
Evaluate results against acceptance criteria in AWS D1.1.

ut_astm.name: Ultrasonic Testing (ASTM)
ut_astm.purpose: Detect internal discontinuities such as laminations and inclusions in plates and welds.
procedure_steps: Select appropriate UT technique, probes, and calibration blocks according to the standard and product specification.
Calibrate the UT instrument using reference blocks with known reflectors.
Apply couplant to the surface and scan the area in a systematic pattern.
Adjust sensitivity and range as required by the standard and procedure.
Record location, depth, and amplitude of relevant indications.
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in ASTM A578 or project specification.

mt_astm.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (ASTM)
mt_astm.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface cracks in ferromagnetic materials.
procedure_steps: Clean the surface to remove oil, grease, scale, and loose particles.
Select magnetization technique (yoke, prod, coil, or head shot) appropriate for geometry.
Apply magnetic field in at least two directions where required.
Apply magnetic particles (dry or wet, visible or fluorescent) during or immediately after magnetization.
Observe for particle indications that outline discontinuities.
Mark and record relevant indications and evaluate against acceptance criteria.

rt_astm.name: Radiographic Testing (ASTM)
rt_astm.purpose: Detect internal volumetric and planar defects using X-ray or gamma radiography.
procedure_steps: Select radiation source, film or digital detector, and exposure parameters according to the procedure.
Position source, object, and film/detector to achieve required coverage and sensitivity.
Place image quality indicators (IQIs) as required by the standard.
Expose the film/detector for the calculated time.
Process film or acquire digital image and evaluate image quality.
Interpret radiographs for relevant indications and compare with acceptance criteria.

coating_thickness_astm.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (ASTM)
coating_thickness_astm.purpose: Measure dry film thickness of coatings on steel substrates.
procedure_steps: Verify that the coating is fully cured according to manufacturer recommendations.
Select appropriate magnetic or eddy current gauge for the substrate and coating type.
Calibrate the gauge using certified shims or calibration plates.
Take multiple readings at each specified location.
Calculate average thickness and compare with specified coating system requirements.
Record results and note any areas below minimum or above maximum thickness.

microstructure_astm.name: Microstructural Examination (ASTM)
microstructure_astm.purpose: Prepare and examine metallographic specimens to assess microstructure.
procedure_steps: Section a representative sample from the area of interest.
Mount the sample if required using a suitable mounting compound.
Grind the surface using progressively finer abrasives according to ASTM E3.
Polish the surface to a mirror finish using appropriate polishing media.
Etch the polished surface with a suitable reagent according to ASTM E407.
Examine the microstructure under an optical microscope and document features such as grain size, phases, and defects.

tensile_en.name: Tensile Test (EN)
tensile_en.purpose: Determine yield strength, tensile strength, and elongation of structural steels per EN.

prep: Select specimen type (round or flat) according to product form and EN ISO 6892-1.
Machine specimen to the specified dimensions and tolerances.
Remove surface defects and burrs without altering the cross-section.
Measure and record original gauge length, width, thickness, and cross-sectional area.
Mark gauge length clearly on the specimen.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and capacity of the tensile testing machine according to EN ISO 7500-1.
Mount the specimen in the grips ensuring correct alignment to avoid bending.
Attach an extensometer or strain measurement device if required for yield and elongation determination.
Select the appropriate test method (A, B, or C) and corresponding strain rate or stress rate as per EN ISO 6892-1.
Zero the load and extensometer before starting the test.
Apply tensile load continuously at the specified rate until fracture.
Record load and extension (or stress and strain) throughout the test.
Determine yield strength (ReH, ReL, or Rp0.2 as applicable), tensile strength (Rm), and elongation at fracture (A or A5).
Fit the broken specimen ends together and measure final gauge length to calculate elongation.
Record fracture appearance and note any unusual behavior (e.g., Lüders bands, discontinuous yielding).

impact_en.name: Charpy V-Notch Impact Test (EN)
impact_en.purpose: Determine impact energy and toughness of structural steels per EN.

prep: Machine Charpy V-notch specimens to the dimensions specified in EN ISO 148-1.
Ensure notch geometry (angle, root radius, depth) complies with the standard.
Check surface finish and remove burrs or machining marks that could affect results.
Mark specimen orientation and notch position relative to rolling direction if required.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and energy range of the pendulum impact testing machine according to EN ISO 148-2.
Set and verify the test temperature using a suitable conditioning medium (e.g., temperature chamber, bath).
Condition specimens at the specified test temperature for sufficient time to achieve uniform temperature.
Place the specimen on the supports with the notch facing away from the striker, as required by the standard.
Release the pendulum to strike and fracture the specimen in a single blow.
Record the absorbed energy from the machine indicator or digital readout.
Repeat the test for the required number of specimens (typically three) at each temperature.
Calculate the average absorbed energy and record individual values.
Report test temperature, average impact energy, and any observed fracture appearance (e.g., percentage shear).

bend_en.name: Bend Test (EN)
bend_en.purpose: Evaluate ductility and ability of structural steels to withstand bending without cracking.

prep: Prepare rectangular bend specimens with specified width and thickness according to EN ISO 7438.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches, sharp corners, or surface defects.
Record specimen thickness, width, and orientation (longitudinal or transverse to rolling direction).

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate bend test method (free bend or guided bend) as specified in EN ISO 7438 or product standard.
Choose the mandrel diameter or former radius based on specimen thickness and required bend angle.
Place the specimen on the supports with the surface to be evaluated in tension during bending.
Apply bending force smoothly until the specified bend angle (often 90° or 180°) is reached.
Release the load and remove the specimen from the fixture.
Inspect the tension surface under good lighting and, if necessary, with magnification.
Record any cracks, tears, or surface discontinuities, including their length and location.
Evaluate the specimen against acceptance criteria defined in the relevant product or welding standard.

hardness_en.name: Hardness Test (EN)
hardness_en.purpose: Determine Brinell or Rockwell hardness of structural steels per EN.

specimen.type: Flat surface on product or sample
prep: Prepare a clean, flat, and smooth surface by grinding or machining.
Ensure the surface is free from scale, rust, oil, and other contaminants.
Verify that the specimen thickness is sufficient for the selected hardness method and load.

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate hardness method (Brinell or Rockwell) and scale according to material and thickness.
For Brinell (EN ISO 6506-1): choose ball diameter and test force as specified for the steel grade and thickness.
Apply the test force gradually, maintain it for the specified dwell time, then remove the force.
Measure the indentation diameter in at least two perpendicular directions and calculate Brinell hardness (HBW).
For Rockwell (EN ISO 6508-1): apply the minor load, then the major load, and read hardness directly from the machine.
Perform multiple measurements at different locations to assess hardness uniformity.
Record hardness values and, if required, convert to other hardness scales using standardized conversion tables.
Compare hardness results with typical or specified ranges for the grade and application.

chemical_en.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (EN)
chemical_en.purpose: Determine chemical composition of structural steels and verify compliance with EN grade limits.

specimen.type: Representative sample from heat or product
prep: Select a representative sample from the heat, plate, section, or coil.
Remove surface contamination, scale, and coatings by machining or grinding.
Ensure the analysis surface is clean, flat, and free from inclusions or defects.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate analytical methods (e.g., optical emission spectroscopy, combustion analysis) according to EN ISO standards.
Calibrate the analytical instrument using certified reference materials with compositions close to the expected range.
Perform blank and control measurements to verify instrument stability.
Carry out the analysis for all required elements (e.g., C, Mn, Si, P, S, alloying elements) according to the chosen method.
Repeat measurements as necessary to ensure repeatability and reliability.
Calculate the average composition for each element and assess measurement uncertainty if required.
Compare the measured composition with the specified limits in the relevant EN product standard (e.g., EN 10025 series).
Prepare and issue a test report or inspection document according to EN 10204 (e.g., 3.1 certificate).

weld_en.name: Weld Procedure Qualification (EN)
weld_en.purpose: Qualify welding procedures for structural steels according to EN requirements.

prep: Prepare test joints (butt or fillet) according to the proposed Welding Procedure Specification (WPS).
Select base materials, filler materials, joint preparation, and thickness range as per EN ISO 15614-1.
Clean joint surfaces and edges to remove rust, scale, oil, and other contaminants.

procedure_steps: Perform welding in accordance with the WPS, controlling preheat, interpass temperature, heat input, and travel speed.
Record all essential and supplementary essential variables during welding (e.g., current, voltage, process, position).
Allow the welded joint to cool under controlled conditions as specified.
Visually inspect the weld for surface defects such as cracks, undercut, porosity, and incomplete fusion.
Perform required non-destructive tests (e.g., MT, UT, RT) on the weld according to relevant EN ISO NDT standards.
Extract mechanical test specimens (tensile, bend, impact) from the weld and heat-affected zone as specified in EN ISO 15614-1.
Conduct tensile tests, bend tests, and impact tests using the appropriate EN ISO methods.
Evaluate all test results against the acceptance criteria in EN ISO 15614-1.
If all requirements are met, approve the welding procedure and issue a Welding Procedure Qualification Record (WPQR).

ut_en.name: Ultrasonic Testing (EN)
ut_en.purpose: Detect and evaluate internal discontinuities in welded joints and plates using ultrasonic testing.

prep: Clean the surface to remove loose scale, rust, paint, and contaminants that may interfere with probe coupling.
Ensure access to both sides of the weld or plate where required by the procedure.
Prepare reference blocks or calibration blocks with known reflectors according to EN ISO 2400 or relevant standards.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate ultrasonic technique (straight beam or angle beam) and probe frequency based on material thickness and defect types.
Calibrate the ultrasonic instrument using reference blocks to set time base, sensitivity, and distance calibration.
Apply suitable couplant to the test surface to ensure good acoustic coupling.
Scan the weld or plate systematically according to the scanning plan defined in EN ISO 17640 or the inspection procedure.
Adjust sensitivity and time-corrected gain (if used) to detect relevant reflectors.
Record the position, depth, and amplitude of all relevant indications.
Classify indications according to their characteristics (e.g., planar, volumetric, root, toe).
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria specified in the applicable product or weld standard (e.g., EN 1090, EN 10160, EN ISO 5817).
Document test results, including calibration details, scanning coverage, indications, and acceptance status.

mt_en.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (EN)
mt_en.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface discontinuities in ferromagnetic structural steels using magnetic particle testing.

specimen.type: Welded joint or surface of structural component
prep: Clean the surface to remove oil, grease, dirt, loose scale, and paint that could mask indications.
Ensure the surface is dry and accessible for magnetization and particle application.
Identify areas of interest such as weld toes, roots, and stress concentration zones.

procedure_steps: Select magnetization technique (yoke, prod, coil, or current flow) appropriate for the geometry and area to be examined.
Verify the adequacy of the magnetic field using field indicators or reference devices as required by EN ISO 17638.
Apply magnetic particles (dry or wet, visible or fluorescent) during or immediately after magnetization.
Ensure that the direction of magnetization is appropriate to detect discontinuities oriented perpendicular to the field.
Inspect the surface under suitable lighting conditions (white light or UV light for fluorescent particles).
Identify and interpret particle accumulations that indicate possible discontinuities.
Mark and record the location, size, and nature of relevant indications.
Demagnetize the component if required and clean off residual particles.
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in the applicable product or weld standard (e.g., EN ISO 5817).

rt_en.name: Radiographic Testing (EN)
rt_en.purpose: Detect internal volumetric and planar defects in welded joints using radiographic testing.

prep: Clean the weld surface to remove contaminants that may affect image quality.
Ensure access for correct positioning of the radiation source and film/detector.
Prepare image quality indicators (IQIs) according to EN ISO 17636-1.

procedure_steps: Select radiation source (X-ray or gamma), film or digital detector, and exposure parameters based on material thickness and required sensitivity.
Position the source, weld, and film/detector according to the geometric requirements of EN ISO 17636-1 (technique A or B).
Place IQIs on the source side or film side as specified to verify image quality.
Shield surrounding areas and ensure radiation safety measures are in place.
Expose the film/detector for the calculated time to achieve the required film density or detector response.
Process the film or acquire digital images according to manufacturer instructions and EN ISO requirements.
Evaluate radiographs for image quality, including IQI visibility, density, and contrast.
Interpret indications (e.g., porosity, slag inclusions, lack of fusion, cracks) and determine their size and location.
Compare indications with acceptance criteria in the applicable product or weld standard and document results.

coating_thickness_en.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (EN context)
coating_thickness_en.purpose: Measure dry film thickness of coatings on structural steel components.

prep: Ensure the coating is fully cured according to the coating manufacturer's recommendations.
Clean the surface to remove loose dust and contaminants that may affect gauge readings.
Verify that the substrate and coating system are suitable for the selected measurement method.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate coating thickness gauge (magnetic, eddy current, or other) according to EN ISO 2808.
Calibrate the gauge using certified calibration foils or reference standards on a similar substrate.
Take multiple measurements at each specified location or area according to the inspection plan.
Discard outliers if justified and calculate the mean thickness for each area.
Compare measured thickness values with the specified minimum and maximum limits for the coating system.
Record all readings, calibration details, and any areas of non-compliance.
If required, repeat measurements after corrective actions (e.g., additional coating).

microstructure_en.name: Microstructural Examination (EN context)
microstructure_en.purpose: Prepare and examine metallographic specimens to assess microstructure, grain size, and weld quality.

specimen.type: Section from plate, section, or weld
prep: Cut a representative sample from the area of interest (e.g., base metal, HAZ, weld metal).
Avoid overheating or altering the microstructure during cutting.
Mount the sample in a suitable mounting medium if required.
Grind the surface using progressively finer abrasives to remove cutting damage.
Polish the surface to a mirror finish using appropriate polishing media.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate etchant for the steel grade (e.g., Nital) and prepare it safely.
Etch the polished surface for a controlled time to reveal the microstructure.
Rinse and dry the specimen carefully after etching.
Examine the microstructure under an optical microscope at suitable magnifications.
Identify phases, grain size, inclusions, and any microstructural anomalies (e.g., banding, coarse grains).
For welds, assess weld metal structure, HAZ width, fusion line, and presence of defects according to EN ISO 17639.
If required, compare grain size with reference charts (e.g., EN ISO 643).
Document micrographs and observations in a metallographic report.

tensile_jis.name: Tensile Test (JIS)
tensile_jis.purpose: Determine yield strength, tensile strength, and elongation of structural steels per JIS at room temperature.

prep: Select specimen type (round, proportional, or non-proportional flat) according to product form and JIS Z 2241.
Machine specimen to the specified dimensions, tolerances, and surface finish in JIS Z 2241.
Avoid overheating or work hardening during machining.
Remove burrs and surface defects without altering the cross-section.
Measure and record original gauge length, width, thickness, and cross-sectional area.
Mark the original gauge length on the specimen as specified in JIS Z 2241.

procedure_steps: Verify tensile testing machine and extensometer calibration according to JIS Z 2241 accuracy requirements.
Mount the specimen in the grips ensuring correct alignment to minimize bending.
Attach an extensometer or strain measurement device if required for yield and elongation determination.
Select the appropriate test speed (stress rate or strain rate) according to JIS Z 2241 for the chosen method.
Zero the load and extensometer before starting the test.
Apply tensile load continuously at the specified rate until fracture.
Record load and extension (or stress and strain) throughout the test as required.
Determine yield point or proof stress (e.g., upper/lower yield, 0.2% proof stress), tensile strength, and elongation at fracture.
Fit the broken specimen ends together and measure final gauge length to calculate elongation.
Record fracture appearance and any discontinuous yielding or Lüders phenomena as required by JIS Z 2241.

impact_jis.name: Charpy V-Notch Impact Test (JIS)
impact_jis.purpose: Determine impact energy and toughness of structural steels per JIS using Charpy V-notch specimens.

prep: Machine Charpy V-notch specimens to the dimensions specified in JIS Z 2242/JIS Z 2248.
Ensure notch geometry (angle, depth, root radius) complies with the standard.
Check surface finish and remove burrs or machining marks that could affect results.
Mark specimen orientation and notch position relative to rolling direction if required by the product standard.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and energy range of the pendulum impact testing machine according to JIS Z 2242/JIS Z 2248.
Set and verify the test temperature using a suitable conditioning medium (e.g., temperature chamber, bath).
Condition specimens at the specified test temperature for sufficient time to achieve uniform temperature.
Place the specimen on the supports with the notch facing away from the striker, as required by JIS.
Release the pendulum to strike and fracture the specimen in a single blow.
Record the absorbed energy from the machine indicator or digital readout.
Repeat the test for the required number of specimens (typically three) at each temperature.
Calculate the average absorbed energy and record individual values.
Report test temperature, average impact energy, and fracture appearance (e.g., percentage shear, brittle/ductile).

bend_jis.name: Bend Test (JIS)
bend_jis.purpose: Evaluate ductility and ability of structural steels to withstand bending without cracking per JIS.

prep: Prepare rectangular bend specimens with specified width and thickness according to JIS Z 2248 or product standard.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches, sharp corners, or surface defects.
Record specimen thickness, width, and orientation (longitudinal or transverse to rolling direction).

procedure_steps: Select the bend test type (face bend, root bend, side bend, free bend) as specified in the relevant JIS product or welding standard.
Choose the mandrel diameter or former radius based on specimen thickness and required bend angle per JIS.
Place the specimen on the supports with the surface to be evaluated in tension during bending.
Apply bending force smoothly until the specified bend angle (often 90° or 180°) is reached.
Release the load and remove the specimen from the fixture.
Inspect the tension surface under good lighting and, if necessary, with magnification.
Record any cracks, tears, or surface discontinuities, including their length and location.
Evaluate the specimen against acceptance criteria defined in the relevant JIS product or welding standard.

hardness_jis.name: Hardness Test (JIS)
hardness_jis.purpose: Determine hardness of structural steels per JIS using Brinell, Vickers, or Rockwell methods.

specimen.type: Flat surface on product or sample
prep: Prepare a clean, flat, and smooth surface by grinding or machining according to the chosen hardness method.
Ensure the surface is free from scale, rust, oil, and other contaminants.
Verify that the specimen thickness is sufficient for the selected hardness method and load as per JIS.

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate hardness method (Brinell, Vickers, or Rockwell) and scale according to material and thickness.
For Brinell (JIS Z 2243): choose ball diameter and test force as specified for the steel grade and thickness.
Apply the test force gradually, maintain it for the specified dwell time, then remove the force.
Measure the indentation diameter (Brinell) or diagonals (Vickers) and calculate hardness according to the relevant JIS standard.
For Rockwell (JIS Z 2245): apply the minor load, then the major load, and read hardness directly from the machine.
Perform multiple measurements at different locations to assess hardness uniformity.
Record hardness values and, if required, convert to other hardness scales using standardized conversion tables.
Compare hardness results with typical or specified ranges for the grade and application.

chemical_jis.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (JIS context)
chemical_jis.purpose: Determine chemical composition of structural steels and verify compliance with JIS grade limits.

specimen.type: Representative sample from heat or product
prep: Select a representative sample from the heat, plate, section, or coil according to the product standard.
Remove surface contamination, scale, and coatings by machining or grinding.
Ensure the analysis surface is clean, flat, and free from inclusions or defects.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate analytical methods (e.g., optical emission spectroscopy, combustion analysis) according to JIS analytical standards.
Calibrate the analytical instrument using certified reference materials with compositions close to the expected range.
Perform blank and control measurements to verify instrument stability.
Carry out the analysis for all required elements (e.g., C, Mn, Si, P, S, alloying elements) according to the chosen method.
Repeat measurements as necessary to ensure repeatability and reliability.
Calculate the average composition for each element and assess measurement uncertainty if required.
Compare the measured composition with the specified limits in JIS G 3101 (SS400) or JIS G 3106 (SM490, SM570).
Prepare and issue a test report or inspection document according to contractual and JIS requirements.

weld_jis.name: Weld Procedure Qualification (JIS context)
weld_jis.purpose: Qualify welding procedures for structural steels according to JIS requirements.

prep: Prepare test joints (butt or fillet) according to the proposed Welding Procedure Specification (WPS).
Select base materials, filler materials, joint preparation, and thickness range as per applicable JIS welding standards.
Clean joint surfaces and edges to remove rust, scale, oil, and other contaminants.

procedure_steps: Perform welding in accordance with the WPS, controlling preheat, interpass temperature, heat input, and travel speed.
Record all essential variables during welding (e.g., current, voltage, process, position, consumable classification).
Allow the welded joint to cool under controlled conditions as specified.
Visually inspect the weld for surface defects such as cracks, undercut, porosity, and incomplete fusion.
Perform required non-destructive tests (e.g., MT, UT, RT) on the weld according to relevant JIS NDT standards.
Extract mechanical test specimens (tensile, bend, impact) from the weld and heat-affected zone as specified.
Conduct tensile tests, bend tests, and impact tests using the appropriate JIS methods.
Evaluate all test results against the acceptance criteria in the applicable JIS welding procedure qualification standard.
If all requirements are met, approve the welding procedure and issue a Welding Procedure Qualification Record (WPQR).

ut_jis.name: Ultrasonic Testing (JIS context)
ut_jis.purpose: Detect and evaluate internal discontinuities in welded joints and plates using ultrasonic testing per JIS.

prep: Clean the surface to remove loose scale, rust, paint, and contaminants that may interfere with probe coupling.
Ensure access to the required surfaces of the weld or plate according to the inspection plan.
Prepare reference blocks or calibration blocks with known reflectors according to applicable JIS UT standards.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate ultrasonic technique (straight beam or angle beam) and probe frequency based on material thickness and defect types.
Calibrate the ultrasonic instrument using reference blocks to set time base, sensitivity, and distance calibration.
Apply suitable couplant to the test surface to ensure good acoustic coupling.
Scan the weld or plate systematically according to the scanning plan defined in the JIS UT procedure.
Adjust sensitivity and time-corrected gain (if used) to detect relevant reflectors.
Record the position, depth, and amplitude of all relevant indications.
Classify indications according to their characteristics (e.g., planar, volumetric, root, toe).
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria specified in the applicable JIS product or weld standard.
Document test results, including calibration details, scanning coverage, indications, and acceptance status.

mt_jis.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (JIS context)
mt_jis.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface discontinuities in ferromagnetic structural steels using magnetic particle testing per JIS.

specimen.type: Welded joint or surface of structural component
prep: Clean the surface to remove oil, grease, dirt, loose scale, and paint that could mask indications.
Ensure the surface is dry and accessible for magnetization and particle application.
Identify areas of interest such as weld toes, roots, and stress concentration zones.

procedure_steps: Select magnetization technique (yoke, prod, coil, or current flow) appropriate for the geometry and area to be examined.
Verify the adequacy of the magnetic field using field indicators or reference devices as required by JIS.
Apply magnetic particles (dry or wet, visible or fluorescent) during or immediately after magnetization.
Ensure that the direction of magnetization is appropriate to detect discontinuities oriented perpendicular to the field.
Inspect the surface under suitable lighting conditions (white light or UV light for fluorescent particles).
Identify and interpret particle accumulations that indicate possible discontinuities.
Mark and record the location, size, and nature of relevant indications.
Demagnetize the component if required and clean off residual particles.
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in the applicable JIS product or weld standard.

rt_jis.name: Radiographic Testing (JIS context)
rt_jis.purpose: Detect internal volumetric and planar defects in welded joints using radiographic testing per JIS.

prep: Clean the weld surface to remove contaminants that may affect image quality.
Ensure access for correct positioning of the radiation source and film/detector.
Prepare image quality indicators (IQIs) according to the relevant JIS radiographic standard.

procedure_steps: Select radiation source (X-ray or gamma), film or digital detector, and exposure parameters based on material thickness and required sensitivity.
Position the source, weld, and film/detector according to the geometric requirements of the applicable JIS radiographic standard.
Place IQIs on the source side or film side as specified to verify image quality.
Shield surrounding areas and ensure radiation safety measures are in place.
Expose the film/detector for the calculated time to achieve the required film density or detector response.
Process the film or acquire digital images according to manufacturer instructions and JIS requirements.
Evaluate radiographs for image quality, including IQI visibility, density, and contrast.
Interpret indications (e.g., porosity, slag inclusions, lack of fusion, cracks) and determine their size and location.
Compare indications with acceptance criteria in the applicable JIS product or weld standard and document results.

microstructure_jis.name: Microstructural Examination (JIS context)
microstructure_jis.purpose: Prepare and examine metallographic specimens to assess microstructure, grain size, and weld quality.

specimen.type: Section from plate, section, or weld
prep: Cut a representative sample from the area of interest (e.g., base metal, HAZ, weld metal).
Avoid overheating or altering the microstructure during cutting.
Mount the sample in a suitable mounting medium if required.
Grind the surface using progressively finer abrasives to remove cutting damage.
Polish the surface to a mirror finish using appropriate polishing media.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate etchant for the steel grade (e.g., Nital) and prepare it safely.
Etch the polished surface for a controlled time to reveal the microstructure.
Rinse and dry the specimen carefully after etching.
Examine the microstructure under an optical microscope at suitable magnifications.
Identify phases, grain size, inclusions, and any microstructural anomalies (e.g., banding, coarse grains).
For welds, assess weld metal structure, HAZ width, fusion line, and presence of defects according to the applicable JIS guidance.
If required, compare grain size with reference charts or specified limits.
Document micrographs and observations in a metallographic report.

coating_thickness_jis.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (JIS context)
coating_thickness_jis.purpose: Measure dry film thickness of coatings on structural steel components according to JIS.

prep: Ensure the coating is fully cured according to the coating manufacturer's recommendations.
Clean the surface to remove loose dust and contaminants that may affect gauge readings.
Verify that the substrate and coating system are suitable for the selected measurement method.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate coating thickness gauge (magnetic, eddy current, or other) according to JIS K 5600.
Calibrate the gauge using certified calibration foils or reference standards on a similar substrate.
Take multiple measurements at each specified location or area according to the inspection plan.
Discard outliers if justified and calculate the mean thickness for each area.
Compare measured thickness values with the specified minimum and maximum limits for the coating system.
Record all readings, calibration details, and any areas of non-compliance.
If required, repeat measurements after corrective actions (e.g., additional coating).

tensile_gb.name: Tensile Test (GB)
tensile_gb.purpose: Determine yield strength, tensile strength, and elongation of metallic materials at room temperature per GB/T 228.1.

prep: Select specimen type (round or flat) according to product form and GB/T 228.1.
Machine specimen to specified dimensions, tolerances, and surface finish in GB/T 228.1.
Avoid overheating or work hardening during machining.
Remove burrs and surface defects without altering cross-section.
Measure and record original gauge length, width, thickness, and cross-sectional area.
Mark gauge length as specified in GB/T 228.1.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration of tensile testing machine and extensometer according to GB/T 228.1.
Mount specimen in grips ensuring correct alignment to minimize bending.
Attach extensometer if required for yield and elongation determination.
Select loading rate (stress rate or strain rate) according to GB/T 228.1 method.
Zero load and extensometer before test.
Apply tensile load continuously at specified rate until fracture.
Record load and extension (or stress and strain) as required.
Determine yield strength or proof strength, tensile strength, and elongation at fracture.
Fit broken specimen ends together and measure final gauge length to calculate elongation.
Record fracture appearance and any discontinuous yielding phenomena.

impact_gb.name: Charpy V-Notch Impact Test (GB)
impact_gb.purpose: Determine Charpy V-notch impact energy and toughness of metallic materials per GB/T 229.

prep: Machine Charpy V-notch specimens to dimensions specified in GB/T 229.
Ensure notch angle, depth, and root radius comply with GB/T 229.
Remove burrs and machining marks that may affect results.
Mark specimen orientation and notch position relative to rolling direction if required by product standard.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and energy range of pendulum impact machine according to GB/T 229.
Set and verify test temperature using appropriate conditioning medium.
Condition specimens at specified temperature for sufficient time to achieve uniform temperature.
Place specimen on supports with notch facing away from striker as required.
Release pendulum to fracture specimen in a single blow.
Record absorbed energy from machine indicator or digital readout.
Test required number of specimens (typically three) at each temperature.
Calculate average absorbed energy and record individual values.
Report test temperature, average impact energy, and fracture appearance (e.g., percentage shear).

bend_gb.name: Bend Test (GB)
bend_gb.purpose: Evaluate ductility and bending performance of metallic materials per GB/T 232.

prep: Prepare rectangular bend specimens with specified width and thickness according to GB/T 232 or product standard.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches and sharp corners.
Record specimen thickness, width, and orientation (longitudinal or transverse).

procedure_steps: Select bend method and mandrel diameter or former radius according to GB/T 232 or product standard.
Place specimen on supports with surface to be evaluated in tension during bending.
Apply bending force smoothly until specified bend angle (often 90° or 180°) is reached.
Release load and remove specimen.
Inspect tension surface under good lighting and, if necessary, magnification.
Record any cracks, tears, or surface discontinuities and their size and location.
Evaluate against acceptance criteria in relevant GB product or welding standard.

hardness_gb.name: Hardness Test (GB)
hardness_gb.purpose: Determine hardness of metallic materials per GB standards using Brinell, Rockwell, or Vickers methods.

specimen.type: Flat surface on product or sample
prep: Prepare clean, flat, smooth surface by grinding or machining.
Ensure surface is free from scale, rust, oil, and contaminants.
Verify specimen thickness is sufficient for selected hardness method and load.

procedure_steps: Select hardness method and scale according to material and thickness.
For Brinell: choose ball diameter and test force per GB/T 231.1.
Apply test force, maintain for specified dwell time, then remove.
Measure indentation diameter (Brinell) or diagonals (Vickers) and calculate hardness.
For Rockwell: apply minor load, then major load, and read hardness directly.
Perform multiple measurements at different locations.
Record hardness values and convert to other scales if required using standardized tables.
Compare with specified or typical hardness ranges for grade and application.

chemical_gb.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (GB)
chemical_gb.purpose: Determine chemical composition of steels and verify compliance with GB grade limits.

specimen.type: Representative sample from heat or product
prep: Select representative sample from heat, plate, section, or coil according to product standard.
Remove surface contamination, scale, and coatings by machining or grinding.
Ensure analysis surface is clean, flat, and free from inclusions or defects.

procedure_steps: Select analytical method (e.g., optical emission spectroscopy, combustion analysis) according to GB/T 4336, GB/T 20123, or GB/T 20125.
Calibrate instrument using certified reference materials.
Perform blank and control measurements to verify stability.
Analyze all required elements (C, Mn, Si, P, S, alloying elements) per method standard.
Repeat measurements as needed to ensure repeatability.
Calculate average composition and assess uncertainty if required.
Compare measured composition with specified limits in GB/T 700 (Q235B), GB/T 1591 (Q345B, Q460).
Issue test report or inspection document according to GB/T 222 and contractual requirements.

ut_gb.name: Ultrasonic Testing (GB)
ut_gb.purpose: Detect internal discontinuities in welded joints and plates using ultrasonic testing per GB.

prep: Clean surface to remove scale, rust, paint, and contaminants.
Ensure access to required surfaces according to inspection plan.
Prepare reference blocks with known reflectors according to GB UT standards.

procedure_steps: Select UT technique (straight beam or angle beam) and probe frequency based on thickness and defect type.
Calibrate instrument using reference blocks to set time base, sensitivity, and distance calibration.
Apply couplant to test surface.
Scan weld or plate systematically according to GB/T 11345 procedure.
Adjust sensitivity and time-corrected gain if used.
Record position, depth, and amplitude of relevant indications.
Classify indications (planar, volumetric, root, toe, etc.).
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in applicable GB product or weld standard.
Document calibration details, coverage, indications, and acceptance status.

mt_gb.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (GB)
mt_gb.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface discontinuities in ferromagnetic steels using MT per GB.

specimen.type: Welded joint or surface of structural component
prep: Clean surface to remove oil, grease, dirt, loose scale, and paint.
Ensure surface is dry and accessible for magnetization and particle application.
Identify areas of interest such as weld toes, roots, and stress concentration zones.

procedure_steps: Select magnetization technique (yoke, prod, coil, current flow) appropriate for geometry.
Verify adequacy of magnetic field using field indicators per GB/T 15822.1.
Apply magnetic particles (dry or wet, visible or fluorescent) during or immediately after magnetization.
Ensure magnetization direction is suitable to detect discontinuities perpendicular to field.
Inspect surface under appropriate lighting (white or UV).
Identify and interpret particle accumulations indicating possible discontinuities.
Mark and record location, size, and nature of relevant indications.
Demagnetize component if required and clean residual particles.
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in applicable GB product or weld standard.

rt_gb.name: Radiographic Testing (GB)
rt_gb.purpose: Detect internal volumetric and planar defects in welded joints using RT per GB.

prep: Clean weld surface to remove contaminants affecting image quality.
Ensure access for correct positioning of radiation source and film/detector.
Prepare image quality indicators (IQIs) according to GB/T 3323.1.

procedure_steps: Select radiation source, film/detector, and exposure parameters based on thickness and sensitivity.
Position source, weld, and film/detector according to geometric requirements of GB/T 3323.1.
Place IQIs as specified to verify image quality.
Implement radiation safety and shielding.
Expose film/detector for calculated time.
Process film or acquire digital images per manufacturer and GB requirements.
Evaluate radiographs for IQI visibility, density, and contrast.
Interpret indications (porosity, slag, lack of fusion, cracks) and determine size and location.
Compare with acceptance criteria in applicable GB product or weld standard and document results.

weld_gb.name: Weld Procedure Qualification (GB)
weld_gb.purpose: Qualify welding procedures for structural steels according to GB requirements.

prep: Prepare butt or fillet test joints according to proposed WPS and GB/T 12467.
Select base materials, filler materials, joint preparation, and thickness range per GB welding standards.
Clean joint surfaces and edges to remove rust, scale, oil, and contaminants.

procedure_steps: Perform welding in accordance with WPS, controlling preheat, interpass temperature, heat input, and travel speed.
Record essential variables (process, current, voltage, position, consumable classification).
Allow welded joint to cool under controlled conditions.
Visually inspect weld for surface defects.
Perform required NDT (MT, UT, RT) according to GB standards.
Extract tensile, bend, and impact specimens from weld and HAZ as specified.
Conduct mechanical tests using appropriate GB methods.
Evaluate results against acceptance criteria in GB/T 12467 or applicable standard.
If compliant, approve procedure and issue WPQR.

microstructure_gb.name: Microstructural Examination (GB)
microstructure_gb.purpose: Prepare and examine metallographic specimens to assess microstructure and grain size per GB.

specimen.type: Section from plate, section, or weld
prep: Cut representative sample from base metal, HAZ, or weld metal.
Avoid overheating or altering microstructure during cutting.
Mount sample if required.
Grind with progressively finer abrasives.
Polish to mirror finish.

procedure_steps: Select suitable etchant (e.g., Nital) and prepare safely.
Etch polished surface for controlled time to reveal microstructure.
Rinse and dry specimen after etching.
Examine under optical microscope at appropriate magnifications.
Identify phases, grain size, inclusions, and anomalies (banding, coarse grains).
For welds, assess weld metal, HAZ width, fusion line, and defects.
Compare grain size with GB/T 6394 or specified limits if required.
Document micrographs and observations in report.

coating_thickness_gb.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (GB)
coating_thickness_gb.purpose: Measure dry film thickness of coatings on steel substrates per GB.

prep: Ensure coating is fully cured per manufacturer.
Clean surface to remove loose dust and contaminants.
Verify substrate and coating are suitable for selected method.

procedure_steps: Select magnetic or eddy current gauge according to GB/T 4956 or GB/T 13452.2.
Calibrate gauge using certified foils or reference standards on similar substrate.
Take multiple measurements at each specified location.
Discard outliers if justified and calculate mean thickness.
Compare measured values with specified coating thickness limits.
Record readings, calibration details, and non-compliant areas.
Repeat measurements after corrective coating if required.

tensile_gost.name: Tensile Test (GOST)
tensile_gost.purpose: Determine tensile properties of metals per GOST 1497.

prep: Prepare specimens according to dimensions and tolerances in GOST 1497.
Avoid overheating and work hardening during machining.
Remove burrs and defects without changing cross-section.
Measure and record original dimensions and gauge length.
Mark gauge length as specified.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration of tensile machine per GOST requirements.
Mount specimen ensuring alignment to minimize bending.
Attach extensometer if required.
Apply load at specified rate according to GOST 1497.
Record load and extension until fracture.
Determine yield strength or proof stress, tensile strength, and elongation.
Measure final gauge length and calculate elongation.
Record fracture appearance.

impact_gost.name: Charpy Impact Test (GOST)
impact_gost.purpose: Determine impact toughness of metals using Charpy specimens per GOST 9454.

prep: Machine specimens to dimensions and notch geometry specified in GOST 9454.
Remove burrs and machining marks.
Mark orientation and notch position if required.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration and energy range of impact machine.
Set and control test temperature as specified.
Condition specimens at test temperature for required time.
Place specimen with notch orientation per GOST 9454.
Fracture specimen with single blow of pendulum.
Record absorbed energy.
Test required number of specimens and calculate average.
Report temperature, average energy, and fracture appearance.

bend_gost.name: Bend Test (GOST)
bend_gost.purpose: Evaluate bending properties of metals per GOST 14019.

prep: Prepare specimens to dimensions specified in GOST 14019 or product standard.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches.
Record thickness, width, and orientation.

procedure_steps: Select mandrel diameter and bend angle according to GOST 14019 or product standard.
Place specimen with required surface in tension.
Bend smoothly to specified angle.
Inspect tension surface for cracks and defects.
Evaluate against acceptance criteria in relevant GOST standard.

hardness_gost.name: Hardness Test (GOST)
hardness_gost.purpose: Determine hardness of metals per GOST methods.

prep: Prepare clean, flat, smooth surface.
Ensure sufficient thickness for selected method and load.
Remove scale, rust, and contaminants.

procedure_steps: Select hardness method and scale according to material and thickness.
Apply load and dwell time per relevant GOST standard.
Measure indentation and calculate hardness.
Perform multiple measurements at different locations.
Record values and convert if required using standard tables.
Compare with specified or typical hardness ranges.

chemical_gost.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (GOST)
chemical_gost.purpose: Determine chemical composition of steels per GOST analytical methods.

prep: Select representative sample from heat or product.
Remove surface contamination and scale.
Prepare clean analysis surface.

procedure_steps: Select analytical method according to relevant part of GOST 22536.
Calibrate instrument with certified reference materials.
Perform blank and control measurements.
Analyze required elements according to method.
Repeat as needed for repeatability.
Calculate average composition.
Compare with limits in GOST 380 (St3sp) and GOST 19281 (09G2S).
Issue composition report per contractual requirements.

ut_gost.name: Ultrasonic Testing (GOST)
ut_gost.purpose: Detect internal defects in welds and products using UT per GOST 14782.

prep: Clean surface for good coupling.
Ensure access to required surfaces.
Prepare reference blocks per GOST 14782.

procedure_steps: Select UT technique and probe frequency.
Calibrate instrument using reference blocks.
Apply couplant and scan according to procedure.
Record indications with position, depth, and amplitude.
Classify indications and evaluate against acceptance criteria in applicable GOST standard.
Document calibration, coverage, and results.

mt_gost.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (GOST)
mt_gost.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface defects in ferromagnetic materials using MT per GOST 21105.

prep: Clean surface from contaminants.
Ensure accessibility for magnetization and particle application.

procedure_steps: Select magnetization method suitable for geometry.
Verify magnetic field adequacy per GOST 21105.
Apply magnetic particles during or immediately after magnetization.
Inspect under appropriate lighting.
Mark and record relevant indications.
Demagnetize and clean if required.
Evaluate indications against acceptance criteria in applicable GOST standard.

rt_gost.name: Radiographic Testing (GOST)
rt_gost.purpose: Detect internal defects in welds using RT per GOST 7512.

prep: Clean weld surface.
Ensure access for source and film/detector.
Prepare IQIs per GOST 7512.

procedure_steps: Select radiation source and exposure parameters.
Position source, weld, and film/detector per GOST 7512.
Place IQIs as specified.
Expose and process film or acquire digital images.
Evaluate image quality and interpret indications.
Compare with acceptance criteria in applicable GOST standard.
Document results.

weld_gost.name: Weld Procedure Qualification (GOST context)
weld_gost.purpose: Qualify welding procedures for structural steels according to GOST requirements.

prep: Prepare test joints according to proposed WPS and relevant GOST welding standards.
Select base and filler materials and joint preparation per standard.
Clean joint surfaces.

procedure_steps: Perform welding per WPS, controlling essential variables.
Record welding parameters.
Visually inspect weld.
Perform required NDT (MT, UT, RT) per GOST standards.
Extract mechanical test specimens from weld and HAZ.
Conduct tensile, bend, and impact tests per GOST methods.
Evaluate results against acceptance criteria in applicable GOST welding qualification standard.
Approve procedure and issue qualification record if compliant.

microstructure_gost.name: Microstructural Examination (GOST)
microstructure_gost.purpose: Determine grain size and assess microstructure per GOST 5639.

specimen.type: Section from plate, section, or weld
prep: Cut representative sample.
Mount if required.
Grind and polish to mirror finish.

procedure_steps: Etch with suitable reagent.
Examine under microscope.
Determine grain size according to GOST 5639.
Assess phases and anomalies.
Document micrographs and results.

coating_thickness_gost.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (GOST)
coating_thickness_gost.purpose: Measure coating thickness on metal substrates per GOST 9.302.

prep: Ensure coating is cured.
Clean surface from loose contaminants.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate thickness gauge.
Calibrate using reference standards.
Measure at specified locations.
Calculate mean thickness and compare with specified limits.
Record results and non-compliant areas.

tensile_asnz.name: Tensile Test (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
tensile_asnz.purpose: Determine yield strength, tensile strength and elongation of hot-rolled structural steels at room temperature.

prep: Select specimen orientation (longitudinal or transverse) as specified in AS/NZS 3678 or AS/NZS 3679.1.
Machine the specimen to the dimensions, tolerances and surface finish specified in AS 1391 for the chosen specimen type.
Avoid overheating or work hardening during machining by using appropriate cutting speeds, feeds and coolant.
Remove burrs and sharp edges without altering the cross-sectional area in the gauge length.
Measure and record the original gauge length, width, thickness and cross-sectional area using calibrated instruments.
Mark the gauge length clearly on the specimen using a fine scribe or punch marks as required by AS 1391.

procedure_steps: Verify that the tensile testing machine and extensometer (if used) are calibrated in accordance with AS 1391.
Select the appropriate load range and ensure the grips are suitable for the specimen geometry.
Mount the specimen in the grips, aligning it carefully to minimise bending stresses during loading.
Attach the extensometer to the gauge length if required for determination of yield or proof stress and modulus.
Zero the load and extensometer readings before commencing the test.
Apply tensile load continuously and without shock at the strain or stress rate specified in AS 1391 for the product form.
Record load and extension (or stress and strain) throughout the test, particularly in the elastic and yielding regions.
Continue loading until the specimen fractures, ensuring that the fracture occurs within the gauge length where possible.
Remove the broken specimen, fit the fracture surfaces together and measure the final gauge length to determine percentage elongation.
Calculate yield strength or proof stress, tensile strength and elongation in accordance with AS 1391 and report the results with specimen orientation and test conditions.

impact_asnz.name: Charpy V-Notch Impact Test (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
impact_asnz.purpose: Determine the Charpy V-notch impact energy and toughness of structural steels at specified test temperatures.

prep: Machine Charpy V-notch specimens to the dimensions specified in AS 1544.2, including length, width and thickness.
Machine the V-notch with the specified angle, depth and root radius, ensuring compliance with AS 1544.2 tolerances.
Remove machining marks and burrs from the notch and specimen surfaces that could influence fracture behaviour.
Mark the rolling direction and notch orientation on the specimen if required by the product standard or test specification.

procedure_steps: Confirm that the pendulum impact testing machine is calibrated and that the energy range is appropriate for the expected absorbed energy.
Set the test temperature using a suitable conditioning medium and verify the temperature with a calibrated thermometer.
Condition the specimens at the specified test temperature for sufficient time to ensure uniform temperature throughout the specimen.
Place the specimen on the supports of the impact machine with the notch facing away from the striker, as required by AS 1544.2.
Raise the pendulum to the initial height and release it to strike and fracture the specimen in a single blow.
Record the absorbed energy from the machine indicator or digital readout for each specimen.
Test the required number of specimens (typically three) at each temperature and calculate the average absorbed energy.
Observe and, if required, record the fracture appearance (e.g. percentage shear, fibrous or brittle fracture).
Report the test temperature, individual and average impact energies, specimen orientation and any observations on fracture appearance.

bend_asnz.name: Bend Test (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
bend_asnz.purpose: Evaluate the ductility and ability of hot-rolled structural steels to withstand bending without cracking.

prep: Prepare rectangular bend specimens with the width and thickness specified in the relevant product or welding standard.
Ensure that the edges are smooth and free from notches, sharp corners and surface defects that could act as stress raisers.
Record the specimen thickness, width and orientation (longitudinal or transverse to the rolling direction).

procedure_steps: Select the bend test type (e.g. face bend, root bend, side bend) and the mandrel diameter or former radius specified for the grade and thickness.
Place the specimen on the supports of the bending fixture with the surface to be evaluated in tension during bending.
Apply bending force smoothly and continuously until the specified bend angle (often 90° or 180°) is reached.
Release the load and remove the specimen from the fixture without causing additional damage.
Inspect the tension surface under good lighting and, if necessary, with low-power magnification.
Record any cracks, tears or surface discontinuities, including their length, location and orientation.
Evaluate the specimen against the acceptance criteria defined in the relevant AS/NZS product or welding standard.

hardness_asnz.name: Hardness Test (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
hardness_asnz.purpose: Determine the hardness of hot-rolled structural steels using Brinell, Vickers or Rockwell methods for quality control and correlation with strength.

specimen.type: Flat surface on product or sample
prep: Prepare a clean, flat and smooth surface by grinding or machining to remove scale, decarburised layers and surface irregularities.
Ensure that the specimen thickness is sufficient for the selected hardness method and test load to avoid influence from the underlying support.

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate hardness method and scale (Brinell, Vickers or Rockwell) based on material thickness and expected hardness range.
Calibrate or verify the hardness testing machine using certified reference blocks in accordance with the relevant standard.
Place the specimen on a firm, stable support to prevent movement during testing.
Apply the test force gradually, maintain it for the specified dwell time, then remove the force as required by the chosen method.
Measure the indentation and calculate the hardness value according to the relevant standard.
Perform multiple measurements at different locations to assess hardness uniformity across the product.
Record all hardness values, test method, scale, load and location, and convert to other hardness scales only using standardized conversion tables when necessary.

chemical_asnz.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
chemical_asnz.purpose: Determine the chemical composition of structural steels and verify compliance with AS/NZS grade limits.

specimen.type: Representative sample from heat or product
prep: Select a representative sample from the heat, plate or section according to the sampling requirements of the product standard.
Remove surface contamination, scale and coatings by machining or grinding to expose clean metal.
Ensure the analysis surface is flat, clean and free from inclusions, porosity or other defects that could affect the result.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate analytical methods (e.g. optical emission spectroscopy, combustion analysis) in accordance with referenced analytical standards.
Calibrate the analytical instrument using certified reference materials with compositions close to the expected range.
Perform blank and control measurements to verify instrument stability and accuracy.
Carry out the analysis for all required elements (C, Mn, Si, P, S and alloying elements) according to the chosen method.
Repeat measurements as necessary to ensure repeatability and reliability of the results.
Calculate the average composition for each element and assess measurement uncertainty if required.
Compare the measured composition with the specified limits in AS/NZS 3678 or AS/NZS 3679.1 for the relevant grade.
Document and report the results, including method, calibration details and any deviations from standard procedures.

ut_asnz.name: Ultrasonic Testing (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
ut_asnz.purpose: Detect and evaluate internal discontinuities in plates and structural sections using ultrasonic methods.

prep: Clean the test surface to remove scale, rust, paint and contaminants that could interfere with probe coupling.
Ensure access to all required scanning surfaces as specified in the test procedure or project specification.

procedure_steps: Select appropriate probes (straight-beam or angle-beam) and test frequencies according to AS 2207 and product thickness.
Calibrate the ultrasonic instrument using reference blocks with known reflectors in accordance with AS 2207.
Apply suitable couplant to the test surface to ensure good acoustic coupling between the probe and the material.
Scan the test area systematically using the specified scanning pattern, overlap and probe movement speed.
Identify and record all relevant indications, noting their position, depth and echo amplitude.
Evaluate indications against the acceptance criteria defined in the applicable standard or project specification.
Document test parameters, calibration details, indications and acceptance decisions in a test report.

mt_asnz.name: Magnetic Particle Testing (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
mt_asnz.purpose: Detect surface and near-surface discontinuities in ferromagnetic steels using magnetic particle methods.

specimen.type: Ferromagnetic surface on product or weld
prep: Clean the surface to remove loose scale, rust, oil, grease and other contaminants.
Ensure that the surface is dry and accessible for magnetisation and particle application.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate magnetisation technique (e.g. yoke, prod, coil or central conductor) based on geometry and defect orientation.
Verify the adequacy of magnetisation using a field indicator or reference device as required by AS 1171.
Apply magnetic particles (dry or wet, visible or fluorescent) to the test area during or immediately after magnetisation.
Observe the surface under suitable lighting (white light for visible particles, UV-A for fluorescent particles) for particle accumulations indicating discontinuities.
Mark and record all relevant indications, noting their size, location and orientation.
Demagnetise the component if required by the specification and clean off residual particles.
Evaluate indications against the acceptance criteria specified in the applicable standard or project specification and document the results.

rt_asnz.name: Radiographic Testing (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
rt_asnz.purpose: Detect and evaluate internal discontinuities in welds and steel components using X-ray or gamma radiography.

prep: Clean the surface of the weld or component to remove dirt, grease and loose scale.
Position the component to allow correct source-to-film geometry as specified in AS 2177.

procedure_steps: Select the appropriate radiation source (X-ray or gamma) and energy level based on material thickness and sensitivity requirements.
Place image quality indicators (IQIs) on the source side or film side as required by AS 2177 to verify radiographic sensitivity.
Position the film or digital detector to achieve the specified coverage and geometric unsharpness.
Set exposure parameters (time, distance, kV or source strength) to obtain the required film density or detector response.
Carry out the exposure while ensuring radiation safety procedures are followed.
Process the film or retrieve digital images and assess image quality, including density, contrast and IQI visibility.
Interpret the radiographs for internal discontinuities such as porosity, slag inclusions, lack of fusion and cracks.
Evaluate indications against the acceptance criteria specified in the applicable standard or project specification and document the results.

weld_asnz.name: Weld Procedure Qualification (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
weld_asnz.purpose: Qualify welding procedures for structural steel by testing welded procedure qualification records (PQRs).

prep: Prepare a test coupon with joint configuration, material grade and thickness representative of production welding.
Weld the coupon in accordance with the proposed welding procedure specification (WPS), recording all essential variables.

procedure_steps: Perform visual inspection of the completed weld to identify surface defects and verify compliance with AS/NZS 1554.1.
Carry out non-destructive testing (e.g. MT and UT or RT) as required by the standard or project specification.
Extract mechanical test specimens (tensile, bend, impact) from the welded coupon in the locations specified by AS/NZS 1554.1.
Conduct tensile tests to determine weld metal and joint strength and compare with base metal requirements.
Perform bend tests (face, root or side) to assess weld ductility and soundness.
Carry out Charpy impact tests where required to verify toughness at specified temperatures.
Evaluate all test results against the acceptance criteria in AS/NZS 1554.1.
If all requirements are met, approve the WPS for production use and document the qualification range and limitations.

microstructure_asnz.name: Microstructure Examination (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
microstructure_asnz.purpose: Examine the microstructure of structural steels to assess grain size, phases, inclusions and heat-affected zones.

prep: Cut a representative sample from the product or weld, avoiding overheating during cutting.
Mount the sample in a suitable mounting medium if required.
Grind and polish the sample through progressively finer abrasives to obtain a scratch-free surface.
Etch the polished surface with an appropriate reagent (e.g. Nital) to reveal the microstructure.

procedure_steps: Examine the etched surface under an optical microscope at suitable magnifications.
Identify and document the phases present and their distribution.
Assess grain size using comparison charts or quantitative methods in accordance with AS 2706 or referenced standards.
Evaluate the presence, type and distribution of non-metallic inclusions if required.
For welds, examine the fusion zone and heat-affected zone for grain coarsening, phase transformations and potential defects.
Capture micrographs of representative areas and annotate them as necessary.
Summarise the microstructural findings and compare them with expected structures for the grade and processing route.

coating_thickness_asnz.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (AS/NZS Hot-Rolled)
coating_thickness_asnz.purpose: Measure the dry film thickness of metallic or organic coatings on steel substrates.

prep: Ensure the coating is fully cured and the surface is clean and free from loose contaminants.
Identify measurement locations in accordance with the relevant coating specification or AS 2331.1.4.

procedure_steps: Select a suitable magnetic or eddy-current coating thickness gauge appropriate for the coating and substrate.
Calibrate the gauge using certified calibration foils or shims on an uncoated substrate of the same type.
Place the probe perpendicular to the coated surface and take individual thickness readings at the specified locations.
Repeat measurements to obtain a representative set of readings for each area.
Calculate the mean dry film thickness and compare it with the specified nominal and minimum values.
Record all readings, calibration details and any deviations from the standard procedure in a test report.

tensile_as1397.name: Tensile Test (AS 1397 Cold-Formed Sheet and Strip)
tensile_as1397.purpose: Determine tensile properties of metallic-coated cold-formed sheet and strip steels in accordance with AS 1397.

prep: Cut tensile specimens from the sheet or strip in the specified orientation (usually longitudinal).
Avoid introducing local heating or deformation during cutting.
Measure and record thickness, width and gauge length using calibrated instruments.
Mark the gauge length clearly on the specimen as per AS 1391.

procedure_steps: Verify calibration of the tensile testing machine and extensometer in accordance with AS 1391.
Mount the specimen in the grips, ensuring correct alignment to minimise bending.
Attach the extensometer if required for yield or proof stress determination.
Apply tensile load at the strain rate specified in AS 1391 for sheet products.
Record yield strength, tensile strength and elongation at fracture.
Report results in accordance with AS 1397, including specimen orientation and test conditions.

bend_as1397.name: Bend Test (AS 1397)
bend_as1397.purpose: Assess the ability of metallic-coated sheet and strip to withstand bending without cracking or coating failure.

prep: Cut bend specimens to the width and length specified in AS 1397 or the product specification.
Ensure edges are smooth and free from notches or burrs.

procedure_steps: Select the bend radius or mandrel diameter specified for the grade and coating class in AS 1397.
Place the specimen in the bending fixture with the coated surface in tension if required.
Bend the specimen smoothly to the specified angle (often 180°) without impact loading.
Inspect the tension surface for cracks, coating flaking or delamination.
Evaluate the result against the bend performance requirements in AS 1397 for the relevant grade and coating.

hardness_as1397.name: Hardness Test (AS 1397 Cold-Formed)
hardness_as1397.purpose: Measure hardness of cold-formed metallic-coated sheet steels for quality control and correlation with strength.

prep: Prepare a smooth, clean surface by light grinding or polishing if necessary, avoiding removal of significant coating thickness.
Ensure sheet thickness is adequate for the selected hardness method and load.

procedure_steps: Select an appropriate hardness method (typically Vickers or Rockwell) suitable for thin sheet.
Calibrate or verify the hardness tester using reference blocks.
Place the sheet on a rigid support to prevent flexing during indentation.
Apply the test force, maintain for the specified dwell time and remove.
Measure the indentation and calculate hardness according to the chosen method.
Record hardness values, test method, load and location.

chemical_as1397.name: Chemical Composition Analysis (AS 1397 Sheet Steels)
chemical_as1397.purpose: Determine the chemical composition of cold-formed sheet steels and verify compliance with AS 1397 grade limits.

prep: Select a representative sample from the coil or sheet.
Remove metallic coating locally if necessary to analyse base steel composition.
Prepare a clean, flat analysis surface.

procedure_steps: Use appropriate analytical methods (e.g. OES, combustion) in accordance with AS 1031 or referenced standards.
Calibrate the instrument with certified reference materials.
Analyse required elements (C, Mn, Si, P, S and others as specified in AS 1397).
Repeat measurements as needed to confirm repeatability.
Compare results with the chemical limits specified for the grade in AS 1397.
Document methods, calibration and results in a test report.

coating_mass_as1397.name: Coating Mass Test (AS 1397)
coating_mass_as1397.purpose: Determine the metallic coating mass (e.g. Z, AZ, AM) on sheet and strip in g/m².

prep: Cut representative samples from the product, avoiding edges unless specified.
Clean the surface to remove oil and loose contaminants.

procedure_steps: Use the single-spot or triple-spot gravimetric method described in AS 1397.
Weigh the sample before stripping the coating.
Strip the metallic coating using the specified chemical reagent without attacking the base steel.
Rinse, dry and reweigh the sample.
Calculate the coating mass per unit area for each side and total, in g/m².
Compare results with the coating class requirements in AS 1397.

coating_thickness_as1397.name: Coating Thickness Measurement (AS 1397)
coating_thickness_as1397.purpose: Measure the thickness of metallic coatings on sheet and strip using non-destructive methods.

prep: Ensure the coating is fully cured and the surface is clean and dry.
Identify measurement locations according to AS 2331.1.4 or product specification.

procedure_steps: Select a suitable magnetic or eddy-current coating thickness gauge.
Calibrate the gauge using certified foils on an uncoated substrate of the same type.
Take multiple readings at each specified location.
Calculate the mean coating thickness and compare with specified values.
Record all readings, calibration details and any deviations from the standard procedure.

adhesion_as1397.name: Coating Adhesion Test (AS 1397 Systems)
adhesion_as1397.purpose: Assess adhesion of metallic or organic coatings applied to sheet steels.

prep: Cut samples to the required size, avoiding damaged or edge areas unless specified.
Clean the surface to remove loose contamination.

procedure_steps: Perform the specified adhesion method (e.g. cross-hatch or pull-off) in accordance with AS 1580.408.1.
Apply the cutting or scoring pattern if cross-hatch is used.
Apply adhesive tape or loading fixture as required by the method.
Remove the tape or apply tensile load and observe coating removal or failure mode.
Classify adhesion performance according to the rating system in AS 1580.408.1.
Record test conditions, method and results.

corrosion_as1397.name: Salt Spray Corrosion Test (AS 1397 Coatings)
corrosion_as1397.purpose: Evaluate the corrosion resistance of metallic coatings on sheet steels using neutral salt spray exposure.

prep: Prepare samples with edges masked or sealed if required.
Clean surfaces without removing or damaging the coating.

procedure_steps: Place specimens in a neutral salt spray chamber configured in accordance with AS 2331.3.1.
Expose specimens for the specified duration or until defined corrosion criteria are reached.
Inspect specimens periodically or at the end of exposure for red rust, blistering or coating breakdown.
Record the extent and type of corrosion and compare with performance requirements.
Document exposure conditions, duration and results in a test report.
